events.text:
>
{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "tested/inspected", "component_conditions": [], "percent_complete": 90, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0, "rate": 0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Final shaft alignment was performed after suction and discharge flanges were bolted up, which is an sequence violation."], "actions": ["Loosen piping flanges to free the pump casing, recheck soft foot, and re-run final laser alignment with piping flanges unbolted.", "Verify cold offset values and re-apply after alignment is redone with piping disconnected."], "rationale": "Per API RP 686, final shaft alignment must be performed with piping flanges disconnected to avoid imposing nozzle loads and casing distortion on the alignment. Bolting up flanges before final alignment can pull the pump out of position, invalidating the as-left readings and leading to premature coupling, bearing, or seal failure in service. Soft foot correction and cold offset application are correct practices, but the sequence error undermines the entire alignment.", "references": ["API RP 686 - Recommended Practices for Machinery Installation and Installation Design, Piping Alignment and Shaft Alignment sections"]}
